name: creating-bauplan-pipelines
description: Creates bauplan data pipeline projects with SQL and Python models. Use when starting a new pipeline, defining DAG transformations, writing models, or setting up bauplan project structure from scratch.
capabilities: Create new bauplan project folders with bauplan_project.yml configuration
Write SQL models for first-node pipeline stages reading from lakehouse tables
Write Python models with decorators for data transformations
Define data quality expectations and validation checks
Configure materialization strategies including REPLACE, APPEND, and OVERWRITE_PARTITIONS
Set up multi-input models joining multiple source tables with I/O pushdown
author: BauplanLabs
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All 165 static findings are false positives: markdown backticks were misidentified as shell execution, version numbers as weak crypto, and CLI documentation as reconnaissance. The skill guides users through bauplan CLI usage safely.
valueStatement: Creating data pipelines from scratch requires understanding DAG architecture, materialization strategies, and project structure. This skill guides you through setting up bauplan projects with proper branch safety, SQL first-node patterns, and Python transformation models.
userTitle: Create bauplan data pipelines with SQL and Python models
limitations: This skill provides guidance only. Actual pipeline execution requires bauplan CLI installed locally
Does not connect to external databases or APIs directly
Does not generate credentials or manage authentication

useCases.title: Set up new pipeline projects
useCases.description: Initialize new bauplan projects with proper DAG structure and SQL/Python models for data transformations

useCases.title: Create transformation models
useCases.description: Write validated transformation models with column specifications and data quality expectations

useCases.title: Build feature pipelines
useCases.description: Create multi-stage pipelines that aggregate data for machine learning feature engineering

promptTemplates.title: New pipeline project
promptTemplates.scenario: Start a new data pipeline from scratch
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a new bauplan pipeline project. I want to read from the taxi_fhvhv and taxi_zones tables in the lakehouse and create a daily_summary output table.

promptTemplates.title: Python transformation
promptTemplates.scenario: Write a Python model with validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Python model that joins trips with zone information. Use I/O pushdown with columns and filter parameters. Add output column validation.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-input model
promptTemplates.scenario: Create a model joining multiple tables
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Python model that takes multiple input tables. Show how to specify multiple bauplan.Model() parameters for a join transformation.

promptTemplates.title: Data quality checks
promptTemplates.prompt: Add data quality expectations to my pipeline. I need checks for no null values, unique IDs, and reasonable value ranges.

bestPractices: Use SQL models only for first nodes that read directly from lakehouse tables
Always specify the columns parameter in @bauplan.model() for output validation
Use columns and filter parameters in bauplan.Model() for I/O pushdown performance
antiPatterns: Using SQL models for complex transformations beyond first nodes
Omitting the columns parameter in @bauplan.model() declarations
Running pipelines on the main branch instead of a development branch

faq.question: Does this skill require bauplan CLI installed?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill guides you through creating pipeline files, but you need bauplan CLI installed locally to run and test pipelines.

faq.question: What are the limits on pipeline complexity?
faq.answer: Bauplan supports arbitrary DAG complexity. Each model produces exactly one output table and can take multiple inputs.

faq.question: Can I integrate with existing data tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill shows how to use DuckDB and Polars in Python models. Bauplan integrates with your existing lakehouse infrastructure.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is read-only documentation. It never accesses your data directly. All operations go through the bauplan CLI which you control.

faq.question: Why is my pipeline failing on dry run?
faq.answer: Check that source tables exist in the lakehouse, columns match your Model() specifications, and you are on a development branch.

faq.question: How does this compare to dbt?
faq.answer: Bauplan uses a similar DAG model but focuses on Python and SQL flexibility with built-in I/O pushdown and strict mode validation.
